title: Living in Boulder vlog 8 - 8 - 2017: Basil the Great

enrichment.summary: Kyle gives a late-night vlog update from Boulder, sharing that he spent the day working through Basil the Great and is on a mission to read all ancient classical texts up through the 600s. He also mentions waiting on new bike pedals before resuming cycling, and plans to tackle Ambrose next.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: committed to reading all books from the ancient classical world through the 600s; calls it 'the best collection in the world'
finds it genuinely interesting and worth knowing, though challenging to get into
believes people who connect history to current events make better decisions

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He didn't do a video yesterday because he was working in telecrash.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He spent a whole day working on tours of Basil the Great.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He recently started working on the Ambros.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He found a page with many links to Ambros, which motivated him to finish it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He wants to compile all books from the ancient classical world up until the 600s.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He is excited about a story related to the Brits and a 3200-page work.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Most of the works he's compiling are not very big.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He hopes there won't be many more gigantic works left to compile.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He called about his bike pedals and expects to get them later this week.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Once he gets the pedals, he will start writing his bike.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He plans to compile the works he already has before getting the pedals.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He believes the collection he's compiling will be the best in the world.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Tomorrow he plans to work on Ambros and possibly the Nag Hammadi library.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He is interested in doing other works through history eventually.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He listened to Scott Cold Riders today and found it interesting.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He thinks the religious church history of the Catholic Church is really interesting because of the amount of material.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He finds it challenging to get into learned religious history but thinks it would be good to know.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He believes knowing history tied into the present time helps people make the right decisions.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He plans to do more videos about how knowing history is beneficial.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He needs to get to sleep soon.

transcript.text: Hello, Living in Boulder Vlog. It's for August 8th, 2017. It's 11.19pm right now.
So, today, yesterday I didn't do a video because I was working until I crashed.
And, um, and because I was working on, what did I do since the last time I did a video?
I did, uh, Gregory of Tours, Basil the Great. It took like a whole day.
Yeah, so I was working on Basil the Great all day today until recently.
And so then I got started on Ambrose.
I came across, um, I had, you know, a list of a few that I wanted to do still.
And then I came across this page that had a whole bunch with links to them.
And so, um, it motivated me to try to just finish it because then I could say I did all of it.
Um, all of the books from the ancient classical world up until, like, the 600s.
I want to do Rome until the fall of Rome and then a little bit after Rome.
Um, when they still, you know, spoke in Latin and it was still Roman culture.
Um, because there was a lot of good material in the 600s.
You had, um, a story that went about the Brits and then Gregory of the Great did a 3200 page one.
So, um, yeah, so, but most of them are not that big.
It's just a little bit of it.
So, hopefully there won't be that many more, like, gigantic ones left.
It'll take a whole day.
Um, because I need to sell my car.
I need to start making some money to make some money.
Um, but I wasn't going to do anything about that until I, uh, started, you know, riding my bike.
So I called today to see what was going on with my pedals.
And they said later this week I would get them.
And so, so I'll just, uh, because once I get them, I'm riding again.
So I'll just, uh, yeah, see how far ahead I can go in these books, you know.
So by the time I do get them, all I have to do is compile them because I already have them.
And, uh, it's going to be a really amazing collection.
Best in the world.
And so, um, tomorrow I'll do Ambrose.
And then I haven't decided, oh, I want to do Nakamadi.
Marcion.
Um, yeah, eventually one day, you know, I'd like to do the other ones, like through history.
But because I was listening today, this guy called Ryan Rees, he's a good, uh, he's like religious story.
That whole religious church, um, history of the Catholic church is really interesting.
Because there's so much material and they're talking about so much.
Um, it's very challenging to get into and learn.
But, uh, it would be good to know.
Um, I'll do other videos about how I would be good to know that kind of stuff.
Um, history in general, tie it up, tie it into the present time.
Um, once people get in the habit of doing that, we'll, we'll all make the right decisions.
Um, so, yeah, that's what I'll do tomorrow.
All I know is Ambrose and then a whole bunch of others.
So, we'll see it.
Um, it's, uh, wide open.
I was listening to...
Oh, yeah, that guy Ryan Rees and then on the, uh, uh, earphones.
What was it?
Can't remember.
Anyway.
Okay, so that's enough talking.
I guess to get to sleep.
Peace out.
